title: Oreophilus lupulus
taxonomy: Oreophilus lupulus is not a well-known plant name. However, Lupulus lupulus is the botanical name for Hops, a perennial plant in the Cannabaceae family. It is also known as Humulus lupulus. This plant is commonly used for brewing beer due to its bitter and aromatic properties. The classification of Humulus lupulus includes the family Cannabaceae and the order Cannabales.
uses: Oreophilus lupulus is not the name of any plant species that I am familiar with. However, I think you may be referring to Humulus lupulus, which is the hop plant. Humulus lupulus has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its flowers and cones have been used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to their sedative and antispasmodic properties. Additionally, its extracts have been used to treat digestive issues and as an antimicrobial agent.
benefits: Oreophilus lupulus, also known as the hop, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its numerous health benefits. The flowers and leaves of the plant contain flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Hop extracts have been shown to have a positive effect on sleep quality, anxiety, and stress relief. properties: Oreophilus lupulus is not a known plant species, however, Humulus lupulus, commonly known as hop, has therapeutic properties. constituents: Oreophilus lupulus, also known as hop, contains various active constituents, including alpha acids, beta acids, and essential oils. The alpha acids are primarily responsible for the bitter flavor and preservative properties of hops. The beta acids, on the other hand, contribute to the plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Essential oils in hops are known for their sedative and anti-inflammatory effects. intro:
There is no information about the plant "Oreophilus lupulus". However, I found a similar plant called "Humulus lupulus", also known as Hops or Common Hops.

Here's a paragraph about Humulus lupulus:

Humulus lupulus, commonly known as Hops or Common Hops, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Cannabaceae family. The health benefits of Humulus lupulus include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Humulus lupulus include flavonoids (such as xanthohumol and isoxanthohumol), essential oils (including myrcene, humulene, and linalool), and prenylated flavonoids like lupulone. The most common herbal preparations made with Humulus lupulus are hop tea, hop extract, and hop-based beer, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce sleep disorders, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety.
